person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Lady Margaret Butler, Lady Boleyn (c. 1454 – 1539) was an Irish noblewoman, the daughter and co-heiress of Thomas Butler, 7th Earl of Ormond. She married Sir William Boleyn and through her eldest son Sir Thomas Boleyn, was the paternal grandmother of Anne Boleyn, second wife of King Henry VIII of England, and great-grandmother of Anne and Henry's daughter, Elizabeth I of England.

Life
She was born at Kilkenny Castle in County Kilkenny, Ireland, the daughter and co-heiress of Thomas Butler, 7th Earl of Ormond and Anne Hankford. Her paternal grandparents were James Butler, 4th Earl of Ormond and Joan de Beauchamp. Her maternal grandparents were Sir Richard Hankford (c. 1397 – 1431) and Anne de Montagu.

She had two sisters: Anne who married Sir James de St. Leger, by whom she had issue, and Elizabeth. Anne and Margaret claimed to be co-heiresses of their father and the Earldom of Ormond, but their cousin, Piers Butler, who had physical control of the Irish estates and the backing of the Irish Council, claimed to be the heir through the direct male line. In 1520, the King granted her a pardon for the alienation of Fritwell Manor, Oxfordshire. The issue wasn't resolved until 1528, by which time Margaret's position was good, with the influence of her granddaughter, then betrothed to Henry VIII, and Margaret's son, Thomas Boleyn's, status as King's adviser.

In 1465 Margaret married Sir William Boleyn, and in total they had ten children. Her son, the ambitious courtier Sir Thomas Boleyn, became the first Earl of Wiltshire and by his marriage to Elizabeth Howard, the Duke of Norfolk's daughter, was the father of Anne Boleyn, Queen Consort of England. Thus, Margaret was great-grandmother to Queen Elizabeth I of England.

Margaret's role in the rise and fall of the Boleyn clan is unknown, although there has been much speculation and theorizing. From c. 1519 onwards she was declared by inquisition to have suffered periods of insanity making her incapable of managing her own estates.

She was the last of the Boleyns to live in Hever Castle as it was given to Anne of Cleves in 1540 sometime after her death. Margaret's lands were claimed by her only surviving grandchild, Mary Boleyn and her husband William Stafford.

Issue
Name	Birth	  Death	Notes
1. Anne Boleyn	18 November 1475 - 31 October 1479	"aged 3 years, 11 months and 13 days", buried with a monumental brass at Blickling.
2. Thomas, 1st Earl of Wiltshire and Ormonde	c. 1477 - 12 March 1539	married Lady Elizabeth Howard; had issue, including Queen Anne Boleyn
3. John	1481 - 1484	 
4. Anne	c. 1483 - 6 January 1555	married John Shelton; had issue
5. Anthony	1483 - 30 September 1493	 
6. Jane	c. 1485 - unknown	married Sir Philip Calthorpe
7. Alice	c. 1487 - 1538	  married Robert Clere
8. Margaret	c. 1489 - unknown	married John Sackville, Esq.
9. William	c. 1491 - 18 December 1571	 
10. James	c. 1493 - 5 December 1561	married Elizabeth Wood
11. Edward	c. 1496 - unknown	married Anne Tempest

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Butler於1454年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 基爾堅尼基爾堅尼時，她的父親Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond28歲，母親Anne Hankford23歲。她於1475年與Sir William Boleyn在英國諾福爾可布利可林結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1539年4月3日在英國英格蘭肯特維享年85歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特維。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Butler于1454年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 基爾堅尼基爾堅尼时，她的父亲Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond28岁，母亲Anne Hankford23岁。她于1475年与Sir William Boleyn在英国諾福爾可布利可林结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1539年4月3日在英国英格兰肯特維享年85岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特維。

nameMeanings.name: Butler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a word that originally denoted a wine steward, usually the chief servant of a medieval household, from Norman French butuiller (Old French bouteillier, Latin buticularius, from buticula ‘bottle’). In the large households of royalty and the most powerful nobility, the title came to denote an officer of high rank and responsibility, only nominally concerned with the supply of wine, if at all. As well as being widespread in England, this is also the surname of an important Irish family, descended from Theobald FitzWalter, who was appointed Chief Butler of Ireland by King Henry II in 1177. It is Gaelicized as de Buitléir.

English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name from Middle English boteler ‘maker of bottles (usually of leather)’, a derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bo(u)teille ‘bottle’ and synonymous with Botelmaker.
Americanized form of French   Bouthillier   (see   Bouteiller  ).

variants.name: Buttler
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Butler  .
German: occupational name for a village tavern owner, from French bouteillier ‘butler’.
Americanized form of German Buttlar: habitational name from a place so named in Thuringia.

variants.name: Bouthillier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Jacques, Normand, Philippe, Yves.
French: variant of   Bouteiller  . This name is sometimes Americanized as   Butler  . Compare   Boutilier  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Butler was born in 1454, in Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Ireland, her father, Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond, was 28 and her mother, Anne Hankford, was 23. She married Sir William Boleyn in 1475, in Blickling, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 3 April 1539, in Hever, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 85, and was buried in Hever, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Butler est née en 1454, à Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlande, son père, Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Anne Hankford, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Boleyn en 1475, à Blickling, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 3 avril 1539, à Hever, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterrée à Hever, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Butler 1454 in Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond 28 und ihre Mutter Anne Hankford 23. Sie hat 1475 in Blickling, Norfolk, England Sir William Boleyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 3. April 1539 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Hever, Kent, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hever, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Butler è nata il nel 1454 nel Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, sua padre, Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Anne Hankford, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Boleyn nel 1475, nel Blickling, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 3 aprile 1539, nel Hever, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 85 anni, e fu sepolta a Hever, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Butlerが1454年にアイルランドコウントイ キルケニーキルケニーで生まれた時，父Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormondは28歳，母Anne Hankfordは23歳であった。1475年にイギリスノーフォルクブリックリングでSir William Boleynと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1539年4月3日にイギリスイギリスケントヘバーで85歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントヘバーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Butler(이)가 1454년 아일랜드 카운티 킬켄이 킬켄이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Hankford(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1475년 영국 노르폴크 블릭링에서 Sir William Boleyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1539년 4월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 헤버에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 헤버에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Butler nasceu em 1454, em Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, seu pai, Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Anne Hankford, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Boleyn em 1475, em Blickling, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 3 de abril de 1539, em Hever, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 85 anos, e foi sepultada em Hever, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Butler родилась в 1454 г. в местности килкенни, коунти килкенни, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Hankford, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Boleyn в 1475 г. в местности бликклинг, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 3 апреля 1539 г. в местности хивир, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 85 лет и была похоронена в местности хивир, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret Butler nació en 1454, en Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, su papá, Thomas Butler 7th Earl of Ormond, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Anne Hankford, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Sir William Boleyn en 1475, en Blickling, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 3 de abril de 1539, en Hever, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultada en Hever, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
